title: helianthus strumosus subsp. tomentosus
intro: Helianthus strumosus subsp. tomentosus, commonly known as prairie sunflower or hairy sunflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Helianthus strumosus subsp. tomentosus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
